# react-crash-guard

> Production-grade React error boundary patterns for SaaS applications.
> Provides global catching, feature isolation, async error bridging, and pluggable error reporting.

## Install

```bash
npm install react-crash-guard
```

Peer dependencies: `react >= 18`, `react-dom >= 18`. Optional: `@sentry/react >= 7` for Sentry integration, `@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch-logs >= 3` for CloudWatch integration.

## When to use this package

Use `react-crash-guard` whenever a React application needs to:
- Prevent one broken component from crashing the entire page
- Catch errors thrown in async callbacks, event handlers, or `useEffect`
- Report errors to Sentry, Datadog, or a custom backend
- Show user-friendly fallback UI based on error type (network, chunk-load, permission, etc.)

## Core API

### GlobalErrorBoundary

Wraps the entire application. Catches all render errors. Tracks retry count to prevent infinite loops.

```tsx
import { GlobalErrorBoundary } from 'react-crash-guard';

<GlobalErrorBoundary
  reporter={sentryReporter}
  fallback={(error, reset) => (
    <div>
      <p>Something went wrong.</p>
      <button onClick={reset}>Try again</button>
    </div>
  )}
>
  <App />
</GlobalErrorBoundary>
```

### FeatureErrorBoundary

Isolates individual features or widgets. Errors stay contained — the rest of the page keeps working.

```tsx
import { FeatureErrorBoundary } from 'react-crash-guard';

<FeatureErrorBoundary
  featureName="UserProfile"
  fallback={<p>Profile unavailable.</p>}
>
  <UserProfile userId={id} />
</FeatureErrorBoundary>
```

### RouteErrorBoundary

Wraps React Router v6 routes. Identifies errors by route name in reports.

```tsx
import { RouteErrorBoundary } from 'react-crash-guard';

<RouteErrorBoundary routeName="/dashboard" fallback={<RouteError />}>
  <Dashboard />
</RouteErrorBoundary>
```

### AsyncErrorBoundary

Boundary variant designed for async data-fetching components.

```tsx
import { AsyncErrorBoundary } from 'react-crash-guard';

<AsyncErrorBoundary fallback={({ reset }) => <RetryButton onClick={reset} />}>
  <DataComponent />
</AsyncErrorBoundary>
```

### useErrorHandler

Bridges errors from async code (setTimeout, fetch, event handlers) to the nearest error boundary.
Must be used inside `ErrorHandlerProvider`.

```tsx
import { ErrorHandlerProvider, useErrorHandler } from 'react-crash-guard';

function DataLoader() {
  const throwError = useErrorHandler();

  async function load() {
    try {
      const data = await fetchData();
      setData(data);
    } catch (err) {
      throwError(err as Error);
    }
  }

  return <button onClick={load}>Load</button>;
}

// Wrap with ErrorHandlerProvider inside a boundary
<FeatureErrorBoundary featureName="DataLoader" fallback={<Fallback />}>
  <ErrorHandlerProvider>
    <DataLoader />
  </ErrorHandlerProvider>
</FeatureErrorBoundary>
```

### useErrorRecovery

Access recovery state and actions from within a boundary's children.

```tsx
import { useErrorRecovery } from 'react-crash-guard';

function RecoveryButton() {
  const { hasError, reset } = useErrorRecovery();
  if (!hasError) return null;
  return <button onClick={reset}>Recover</button>;
}
```

### SentryReporter

Built-in Sentry integration. Pass to any boundary's `reporter` prop.

```tsx
import { SentryReporter } from 'react-crash-guard';
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/react';

Sentry.init({ dsn: '...' });
const reporter = new SentryReporter(Sentry);

<GlobalErrorBoundary reporter={reporter}>
  <App />
</GlobalErrorBoundary>
```

### CloudWatchReporter

Built-in AWS CloudWatch Logs integration. Sends a structured JSON log event to CloudWatch on every boundary-caught error. No hard dependency on the AWS SDK — pass a `putLogEvents` callback that wraps `PutLogEventsCommand`, so you control the client and credentials.

```tsx
import {
  CloudWatchLogsClient,
  PutLogEventsCommand,
} from '@aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch-logs';
import { GlobalErrorBoundary, CloudWatchReporter } from 'react-crash-guard';

const client = new CloudWatchLogsClient({ region: 'us-east-1' });

const reporter = new CloudWatchReporter({
  logGroupName: '/myapp/errors',
  logStreamName: 'production',
  putLogEvents: async (logGroupName, logStreamName, logEvents) => {
    await client.send(new PutLogEventsCommand({ logGroupName, logStreamName, logEvents }));
  },
  service: 'my-frontend',    // included in every log entry
  environment: 'production', // included in every log entry
});

<GlobalErrorBoundary reporter={reporter}>
  <App />
</GlobalErrorBoundary>
```

Each crash writes one CloudWatch log event whose `message` is a JSON string with these fields:
`level`, `timestamp` (ISO 8601), `message`, `errorName`, `stack`, `boundaryName`, `routeName`,
`featureName`, `componentStack`, `userId`, `service`, `environment`, plus any fields from `context.extra`.
Undefined fields are omitted. Reporter silently no-ops if `putLogEvents` throws.

### ConsoleReporter

Development reporter that logs errors to the console.

```tsx
import { ConsoleReporter } from 'react-crash-guard';
const reporter = new ConsoleReporter('[MyApp]');
```

### classifyError

Classifies an error into a structured type with a user-friendly message.

```tsx
import { classifyError } from 'react-crash-guard';

const classified = classifyError(error);
// { type: 'network' | 'chunk-load' | 'permission' | 'render' | 'unknown',
//   recoverable: boolean, userMessage: string, retryable: boolean }
```

### serializeError

Serializes an Error to a plain object safe for JSON logging.

```tsx
import { serializeError } from 'react-crash-guard';
const plain = serializeError(error); // { name, message, stack }
```

## Complete app setup example

```tsx
import {
  GlobalErrorBoundary,
  ErrorHandlerProvider,
  FeatureErrorBoundary,
  SentryReporter,
  ConsoleReporter,
} from 'react-crash-guard';
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/react';

Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN });

const reporter = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'
  ? new SentryReporter(Sentry)
  : new ConsoleReporter('[App]');

function Root() {
  return (
    <GlobalErrorBoundary
      reporter={reporter}
      fallback={(error, reset) => (
        <ErrorScreen error={error} onReset={reset} />
      )}
    >
      <ErrorHandlerProvider>
        <App />
      </ErrorHandlerProvider>
    </GlobalErrorBoundary>
  );
}
```

## TypeScript types

All props interfaces are exported: `GlobalErrorBoundaryProps`, `RouteErrorBoundaryProps`,
`FeatureErrorBoundaryProps`, `ErrorContext`, `ErrorFallback`, `ClassifiedError`, `ErrorBoundaryRef`.

Reporter types: `SentryReporterOptions`, `CloudWatchReporterOptions`, `CloudWatchLogEvent`, `PutLogEventsFn`.

Reporter interface:

```ts
interface ErrorReporter {
  report(error: Error, context: ErrorContext): void | Promise<void>;
}
```

## Error classification types

| type | Triggered by | recoverable | retryable |
|------|-------------|-------------|-----------|
| `network` | "network", "fetch", "xhr" in message | true | true |
| `chunk-load` | "loading chunk", "loading css chunk" | true | false |
| `permission` | "permission", "forbidden", "unauthorized" | false | false |
| `render` | other React render errors | true | true |
| `unknown` | anything else | true | true |
